title: Field names should follow a naming convention
description: Field names should comply with a naming conventionAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?More InfoA naming convention in software development is a set of guidelines for naming code elements like variables, functions, and classes.The goal of a naming convention is to make the code more readable and understandable, which makes it easier to maintain and debug. It also ensures consistency in the code, especially when multiple developers are working on the same project.This rule checks that field names match a provided regular expression.Using the regular expression^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$, the noncompliant code below:class MyClass { int my_field; // Noncompliant };Should be replaced with:class MyClass { int myField; // Compliant };Available
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Field names should follow a naming convention - field should use camelCase instead of underscore_notation or ALL_CAPS
